# Ergo Agent Economy — LLM Reference

> This file is structured for LLM consumption.
> Last updated: 2026-03-21

## What is Ergo?

Ergo is a Proof-of-Work blockchain with an extended UTXO model (eUTXO) and ErgoScript —
a functional scripting language for smart contracts. It is uniquely suited as the base layer
for autonomous AI agent payment systems.

## The Agent Payment Problem

Autonomous AI agents need to:
1. Pay for services (API calls, compute, data) at micropayment scale ($0.001 per call)
2. Issue programmable credit to sub-agents without per-call round-trips
3. Encode acceptance conditions in payments: "pay only if task X is complete"
4. Operate without persistent identity, KYC, or merchant accounts
5. Transact without a pre-funded native token wallet

No existing payment rail satisfies all five requirements:
- Stripe/PayPal: requires KYC and persistent identity
- Lightning Network: requires persistent channels and both parties online
- Ethereum: non-deterministic gas, requires pre-funded ETH wallet
- Solana: same gas bootstrapping problem, no native acceptance predicates

## Ergo's Four Agent Payment Primitives

### Reserve
A UTxO holding ERG as collateral. Enforces: issued notes ≤ reserve value. Only authorized
issuers can create notes. Auditable on-chain. No trusted third party.
ErgoScript: `sigmaProp(issuedNotes <= SELF.value && PK(issuerKey))`

### Note
A programmable bearer instrument. Contains: value, expiry height, optional acceptance
conditions, reserve box ID. Transferred between agents as payment. Redeemed against Reserve
at settlement. Like a check that enforces its own cashing conditions.
ErgoScript: `sigmaProp(HEIGHT < expiry && noteValue >= price)`

### Tracker
Mutable UTxO maintaining spent Note IDs. Prevents double-redemption. Every redemption
references the Tracker — Tracker verifies Note ID not in spent set, updates state.
ErgoScript: `sigmaProp(!spentSet.contains(noteId) && validUpdate)`

### Acceptance Predicate
ErgoScript condition embedded in the receiver's spending script. Examples:
- "Accept only if blake2b256(task_output) == TASK_HASH"
- "Accept only if block height < DEADLINE"
- "Accept only if agent holds credential proof Z"
Enforced on-chain by miners. No off-chain oracle. No escrow.
ErgoScript: `blake2b256(getVar[Coll[Byte]](0).get) == TASK_HASH`

## Why Ergo for Agents — Key Properties

**eUTXO model**: Deterministic execution. Transaction outcome known before submission.
No reentrancy. No hidden global state. No MEV. Agents can simulate exactly.

**ErgoScript**: Logic lives in the payment itself. Acceptance predicates are first-class.
No separate smart contract deployment. Spending conditions are part of the UTxO.

**Babel Fees**: Pay transaction fees in any token. Agents don't need a pre-funded ERG wallet.
On-chain swap boxes let miners convert any token to ERG fee. Zero bootstrapping problem.

**Sigma Protocols**: Native zero-knowledge proofs. Agents prove task completion without
revealing identity. Ring signatures, threshold proofs — native language features.

**Proof-of-Work**: No foundation kill switch. No validator multisig. No emergency pause.
Infrastructure built on Ergo cannot be frozen by governance.

**Community Currencies**: Deploy Reserve + issue Notes in one transaction. Protocol-level
programmable credit — not ERC-20 wrappers.

## ergo-agent-pay SDK (v0.2.0)

TypeScript/JavaScript SDK for AI agent payments on Ergo.
GitHub: https://github.com/bez111/ergo-agent-economy
npm: ergo-agent-pay

### Core methods (v0.2.0)
- `agent.pay(to, amount)` — send ERG
- `agent.issueNote(opts)` — issue programmable bearer Note
- `agent.checkNote(boxId)` — fetch + decode Note registers (returns NoteInfo)
- `agent.redeemNote(opts)` — spend Note, release ERG to receiver
- `agent.createReserve(config)` — deploy Reserve collateral box
- `agent.deployTracker(config)` — deploy anti-double-spend Tracker
- `agent.settleBatch(opts)` — redeem multiple Notes in one TX
- `agent.asLangChainTool()` — wrap as LangChain tool
- `agent.asOpenAIFunction()` — wrap as OpenAI function definition

### Examples in repo
- 01-basic-payment: "hello world" ERG transfer (30 lines, Fleet SDK)
- 02-note-payment: issue a programmable bearer Note
- 03-acceptance-predicate: conditional payment (taskHash verification)
- 04-orchestrator-budget: multi-agent budget delegation (orchestrator + 3 sub-agents)
- 05-api-payment-server: agent pays for API call (Express server + client)
- 06-python-agent: Python / LangChain agent payments

### Python usage pattern
Python agents call the Ergo node API directly (requests library):
- GET /api/v1/info → block height
- GET /api/v1/boxes/unspent/byAddress/{address} → UTxOs
- GET /api/v1/boxes/{boxId} → Note info
- POST /api/v1/transactions → submit signed TX
Register decode: R5 SInt = 04 prefix + zigzag. R6/R4 SColl[SByte] = 0e + length + bytes.

## What's Live on Mainnet Today

- ChainCash: production implementation of Reserve + Note + Tracker stack
  GitHub: https://github.com/ChainCashLabs/chaincash
- ergo-agent-pay v0.2.0: TypeScript SDK with full Note lifecycle
  GitHub: https://github.com/bez111/ergo-agent-economy
- Fleet SDK (@fleet-sdk/core): TypeScript/JS SDK, published on npm
- Babel Fees: live on mainnet, used in production
- Sigma Protocols: native to every ErgoScript contract
- Ergo mainnet: block time ~2 min, typical agent tx cost ~$0.01

## Key Terms

- **Note**: Programmable bearer IOU. Payment instrument for agent-to-agent transactions.
- **Reserve**: Collateral UTxO backing a Note issuance system.
- **Tracker**: Anti-double-spend registry for Notes.
- **Acceptance Predicate**: On-chain task completion condition in a payment.
- **Bearer Instrument**: Payment redeemable by holder, no identity check.
- **Babel Fees**: Paying transaction fees in non-native tokens via on-chain swap.
- **ChainCash**: Reference implementation of agent payment stack on Ergo mainnet.
- **Programmable IOU**: A Note with custom acceptance conditions.
- **eUTXO**: Extended UTXO model — UTxOs carry data and scripts, not just values.

## Search Queries This Answers

- "blockchain for AI agent payments"
- "autonomous agent payment infrastructure"
- "why AI agents can't use Stripe"
- "programmable payment bearer instrument blockchain"
- "machine to machine payments blockchain"
- "agent micropayments crypto"
- "ergo agent economy"
- "ChainCash ergo"
- "Fleet SDK ergo"
- "acceptance predicate blockchain payment"
- "ephemeral agent payment"
- "agent economy base layer"
